metadata.name: ISO 31000:2018 Risk Management
metadata.description: ISO 31000:2018 provides guidelines on managing risk faced by organizations. The application of these guidelines can be customized to any organization and its context. It provides a common approach to managing risk, and provides guidance on principles, framework, and process for managing risk.
metadata.classification_reasoning: ISO 31000 is the internationally recognized risk management standard providing universal guidance applicable to all organizations and risk types.
metadata.framework_purpose: To provide principles, a framework, and a process for managing risk in a manner that is transparent, systematic, and credible.
metadata.framework_scope: All types of organizations and all types of risks. The standard is not specific to any industry or sector.
framework_objectives: Increase the likelihood of achieving organizational objectives
Improve identification of opportunities and threats
Establish a reliable basis for decision-making
metadata.target_audience: Risk Managers, CROs, Board Members, Senior Management, Internal Auditors, Compliance Officers, and all functions involved in risk management
adoption_approach: Establish commitment and leadership for risk management
Integrate risk management into organizational processes

controls.title: Integrated Risk Management
controls.description: Risk management shall be integrated into all organizational activities.
controls.full_text: Risk management is not a standalone activity separate from main organizational activities and decision-making. It is integrated and embedded into all organizational processes, including strategic planning, project management, operations, and performance management.

controls.title: Structured and Comprehensive Approach
controls.description: A structured and comprehensive approach to risk management contributes to consistent and comparable results.
controls.full_text: Apply risk management in a structured, comprehensive, and systematic manner. Use a consistent methodology across the organization. Comprehensive risk identification, assessment, and treatment ensures no significant risk is overlooked.

controls.title: Customized Risk Management
controls.description: The risk management framework and process are customized and proportionate to the organization's external and internal context.
controls.full_text: Risk management is tailored to the organization's specific context, objectives, culture, and risk profile. A one-size-fits-all approach is not appropriate. The methodology should be proportionate to the complexity and nature of the organization.
evidence_requirements: Risk appetite calibrated to organizational context

controls.title: Stakeholder Inclusion
controls.description: Appropriate and timely involvement of stakeholders enables their knowledge, views, and perceptions to be considered.
controls.full_text: Involve relevant stakeholders in risk management processes. Stakeholder engagement improves risk identification, supports risk acceptance decisions, and enhances credibility of risk assessments. Communication and consultation with stakeholders is ongoing throughout the risk management process.

controls.title: Dynamic Risk Management
controls.description: Risks can emerge, change, or disappear as an organization's external and internal context changes. Risk management anticipates, detects, acknowledges, and responds to those changes.
controls.full_text: Risk management must be dynamic and responsive to change. The risk environment continuously evolves due to external events, organizational changes, and emerging threats. Risk identification and assessment processes must be adaptive and updated regularly.

controls.title: Leadership and Commitment
controls.description: Management and governing bodies establish and sustain their commitment to risk management.
controls.full_text: Top management and governing bodies demonstrate commitment to risk management by integrating it into governance structures, defining risk appetite, ensuring adequate resources, allocating responsibility, and reviewing risk management performance. Leadership sets the tone for risk culture.

controls.title: Integration into the Organization
controls.description: The organization integrates risk management throughout the organization via an appropriate governance structure.
controls.full_text: Design and implement a framework that integrates risk management processes into all levels of the organization. This includes integrating risk management into governance policies, strategies, organizational design, and reporting. All functions share responsibility for risk management.
evidence_requirements: Risk management policy and framework document

controls.title: Design of Framework
controls.description: The organization designs a framework aligned with internal and external context.
controls.full_text: Design a risk management framework appropriate to the organization's context. The framework defines roles and responsibilities, processes, tools, communication, and reporting. It must align with governance requirements and be proportionate to the organization's complexity.

controls.title: Evaluation and Improvement
controls.description: The organization evaluates and improves the suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness of the risk management framework.
controls.full_text: Periodically evaluate whether the risk management framework is achieving its intended outcomes. Identify gaps and opportunities for improvement. Update the framework based on evaluation results. Demonstrate continual improvement of risk management maturity.

controls.title: Context Establishment
controls.description: The organization defines external and internal context for risk management, including risk criteria.
controls.full_text: Establish the external context (regulatory, political, economic, social, technological, environmental factors) and internal context (organizational objectives, culture, capabilities, processes). Define risk criteria including risk appetite, tolerance thresholds, and acceptable risk levels.

controls.title: Risk Assessment
controls.description: Risk assessment encompasses risk identification, risk analysis, and risk evaluation.
controls.full_text: Conduct comprehensive risk assessments comprising risk identification (what can happen, when, where, why, and how), risk analysis (consequences, likelihood, existing controls, residual risk), and risk evaluation (compare against criteria, prioritize). Use qualitative, semi-quantitative, or quantitative approaches as appropriate.

controls.title: Risk Treatment
controls.description: Risk treatment involves selecting and implementing options for addressing risk.
controls.full_text: Select and implement risk treatment options: modifying likelihood or consequence (controls), avoiding risk, sharing risk (insurance, contracts), accepting risk. Develop risk treatment plans with responsible parties, timelines, resources, and performance measures. Evaluate residual risk after treatment.

controls.title: Monitoring and Review
controls.description: The purpose of monitoring and review is to assure and improve the quality and effectiveness of process design, implementation, and outcomes.
controls.full_text: Continuously monitor and periodically review risk management processes and outcomes. Monitor: the external and internal context, risk exposures and the operation of risk controls, and progress of treatment plans. Review should be planned and periodic. Results are used to improve the framework.

controls.title: Recording and Reporting
controls.description: The risk management process and its outcomes should be documented and reported through appropriate mechanisms.
controls.full_text: Maintain documented records of risk management activities including risk assessments, treatment plans, monitoring results, and decisions. Report risk information to appropriate stakeholders at appropriate times. Reporting supports accountability, decision-making, and continual improvement.
evidence_requirements: Risk management reports to board and management

controls.title: Communication and Consultation
controls.description: The aim of communication and consultation is to assist relevant stakeholders in understanding risk and the basis on which decisions are made.
controls.full_text: Establish and implement communication and consultation plans for risk management. Communicate risk information to stakeholders to support decision-making. Consult stakeholders to obtain input for risk identification and assessment. External communication includes risk disclosures to investors and regulators.

controls.title: Risk Culture Development
controls.description: The organization develops and nurtures a risk culture that enables open communication about risks at all levels.
controls.full_text: Foster a risk-aware culture where employees feel empowered to identify, report, and escalate risks without fear. Risk culture is reinforced through leadership behavior, communication, training, and recognition. Risk culture is assessed periodically through surveys and assessments.
evidence_requirements: Risk culture surveys and assessment results

controls.title: Risk Portfolio and Aggregation
controls.description: The organization maintains a portfolio view of risks and assesses their combined and correlated impact.
controls.full_text: Maintain a comprehensive enterprise risk portfolio. Analyze how individual risks correlate and aggregate. Identify concentration risks where multiple risks could materialize simultaneously. Report the aggregated risk position to the board relative to risk appetite.
evidence_requirements: Enterprise risk register with all categories

controls.title: Emerging Risk Identification
controls.description: The organization proactively identifies and monitors emerging risks that may materialize in the medium to long term.
controls.full_text: Establish processes to identify and monitor emerging risks on the horizon. Sources include horizon scanning, executive surveys, scenario analysis, and external expert input. Emerging risks are tracked in the risk register with early warning indicators. Board is informed of emerging risks.
